name: Focaccia, Provolone-Oregano
recipeIngredient: 6 oz provolone cheese (1 1/2 Cups) - cut into 1/4" cubes
2 tbsp fresh oregano or 2 tsp. dried oregano
recipeInstructions:
1. Gently cook the garlic in the oil for 3-5 minutes, just until it begins to brown slightly. Remove from heat and cool.
2. In a large bowl, soften the yeast in the warm water. Add the sugar, salt, 2 tbsp cooled garlic oil, whole wheat flour, semolina flour, provolone cheese, and oregano. Beat vigorously for 2 minutes.
3. Gradually add all-purpose flour (1/4 cup at a time) until the dough mass forms and pulls away from the sides of the bowl. Knead for 8-10 minutes, adding flour if necessary, until the dough is smooth and elastic and blisters have started to form on the surface.
4. Place dough in an oiled bowl, turning to coat the top of the bowl. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
5. Turn the dough onto a work surface, pressing into a 14" circle. Cover and let rise for 20 minutes.
6. Dimple the dough with your fingertips, pressing down to the bottom of the pan. Cover and let rise for another 20 minutes.
7. About 15 minutes before the end of the rising time, preheat the oven to 400°F. Just before baking, drizzle the top of the dough with the remaining garlic oil mixture, allowing it to puddle in the dimples.
8. Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown (internal temperature should reach 190°F). Immediately remove the bread from the pan and place on a rack. Focaccia is best eaten warm or at room temperature.
